commit.message:
docs: restore manifold theorem statements in README

Add the generic and numerical complex-manifold theorem statements back to the
README's theorem section so the class-map-first architecture is documented at
both the local-homeomorphism and manifold levels.

- show `StabilityCondition.WithClassMap.existsComplexManifoldOnConnectedComponent`
- show `NumericalStabilityCondition.existsComplexManifoldOnConnectedComponent`
- keep the surrounding explanation aligned with the current class-map-first API

Build and lint passed:
- lake exe cache get
- lake build BridgelandStability
- lake exe runLinter

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Install CA certificates for HTTPS probes

Bake CA roots into the container so HTTPS checks work reliably in Docker, and document the TLS certificate requirement in the README.

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

refactor: make the stability-condition API class-map-first

Promote `PreStabilityCondition.WithClassMap` and `StabilityCondition.WithClassMap`
to the primary generic API for class maps `v : K₀ C →+ Λ`, and re-express the
numerical layer as the canonical specialization to the Euler numerical quotient.

- define the `WithClassMap` topology and generic local-homeomorphism package
- keep `ClassMapStabilityCondition` only as the comparison subtype for the
  surjective factorization bridge
- make `NumericalStabilityCondition` the `WithClassMap` specialization to
  `numericalQuotientMap`
- update the manifold layer to expose public theorems on `WithClassMap`
- rename the old heart-equivalence prestability helper to `PhasePackage` and
  add the reusable `Slicing.ext` lemma used by the deformation proofs
- sync the README and audit artifacts to the new architecture
- keep the paper-facing Theorem 1.2 proposition-object as an explicit wrapper
  over the generic class-map-first layer

Build and lint passed:
- lake exe cache get
- lake build BridgelandStability
- lake exe runLinter

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Fix YouTube scrobbling: proper covers, artist parsing, multi-tab, restart state

YouTube improvements:
- Fetch Last.fm album art instead of video thumbnails for YouTube music
- Also fetches album name from track.getInfo for proper scrobble metadata
- Falls back to YouTube thumbnail only when Last.fm has no art
- Parse "Track - Artist" reversed titles by validating against Last.fm artist pages
- UGC videos parse artist from title, not channel name
- Strip (Official Video), [MV], (Audio) etc. from titles
- Multi-tab detection: scans ALL music tabs, picks newest changed one

Album detail:
- Filter out tiny/placeholder artwork URLs (34s, 64s, default star)
- Save artwork button (current cover → NSSavePanel)
- Save All button (all covers → folder picker, numbered files)

Scrobble timing:
- On app restart, seed accumulated time from track's elapsed position
- Progress bar and scrobble ring start at correct position, not zero
- Auto-scrobble if already past threshold on restart

API tracking wired into all 6 services via URLSession.trackedData()

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
[Android] Add new BpkSearchInputControl component (#2626)

Add BpkSearchInputControl component with docking support

Introduce BpkSearchInputControl — a non-editable search field with
prefix support (None, Text, Icon), focus state, clear action, and
docking (Float/Top/Middle/Bottom) for stacking controls with shared
corner rounding. Available in OnDefault and OnContrast styles.

- Extract BpkStaticFieldImpl for Select and SearchInputSummary-ReadOnly
- Extract shared field utilities into BpkFieldImplShared
- Focused border: 2dp blue with white gap; no border when unfocused
- Revert BpkSearchInputSummary to original API (no Docking)
- Add stories, snapshot tests, and README

commit.message:
Add tabbed settings, radar chart, API call tracker, live metrics

Settings redesign:
- Three tabs: General, Integrations, Developer
- General: account, scrobble behavior, appearance, quit
- Integrations: app connectors with icons/bundle IDs, API keys, supported services list
- Developer: full diagnostic dashboard

Developer tab:
- Radar chart for scoring weights (5-axis with threshold ring)
- API call tracker across all 6 services — total/error/success counts
- Per-service bar chart sorted by call volume
- Timing table: avg/min/max ms per service
- Response time sparklines for top 3 services
- Source trust horizontal bar chart
- Memory/CPU circular gauges + sparklines (from MetricsService)
- Full diagnostics: MediaRemote, virtual memory, CPU time, threads
- Cache management with clear buttons

API tracking:
- APITracker.shared records every outgoing API call
- URLSession.trackedData() extension auto-tracks all services
- Wired into Last.fm, Discogs, MusicBrainz, iTunes, Wikidata, YouTube Music

Also fixes:
- YouTube duration from API (lengthSeconds) — proper scrobble timing
- Scrobble threshold: 50% of known duration, 60s for unknown, min 30s floor
- History refreshes immediately after successful scrobble
- Menu bar reads from MediaRemote bridge for instant track updates
- Progress bar updates every second from accumulated play time

perf(core): reduce repeated permission and replay work (#50)

Summary:
- cache permission decisions by component type during filtering and
  reuse the allowed-id set across root and subtree pruning
- compute update replay fingerprints once per normalized update
  instead of re-hashing on duplicate checks and apply
- simplify binding conflict detection to direct set intersections and
  add a regression test for repeated permission checks

Rationale:
- cut repeated authorizer and permission-policy work in a render-time
  hot path without changing the filtering contract
- remove redundant hashing from stream replay bookkeeping while
  preserving idempotency and conflict detection semantics
- keep the cleanup targeted to clarity and hot-path efficiency with
  coverage for the new permission memoization behavior

Tests:
- mix test test/json_liveview_render/permissions_test.exs test/json_liveview_render/bindings_test.exs test/json_liveview_render/stream_test.exs
- mix test

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix(browser): bound all Chrome 146 live-attach paths + fix test flakiness (#88)

Extends v0.2.30's Chrome 146 handling to ALL live-attach entry points:

- browser check: daemon cleanup + Allow dialog message on failure
- browser attach (CDP + auto-connect): bail with Allow hint
- browser login --cdp: daemon cleanup + Allow hint before cookie fallback
- recipe --cdp: daemon cleanup + Allow hint on explicit CDP failure

Also bounds try_auto_connect() and try_cdp_attach() in browser.rs:
- verify_auth_cdp now uses bounded command timeouts (30s)
- try_auto_connect short-circuits on healthy daemon socket
- try_cdp_attach preserves Allow-dialog errors without wrapping

Test fixes (Codex):
- resolve_agent_browser() env override checked on every call (was cached)
- Poison-recovering mutex lock (into_inner) + EnvVarGuard for cleanup
- Unique socket dir uses pid + nanos + atomic counter (no collisions)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add scrobble cache with retry and persistence

ScrobbleCache actor:
- Queues failed scrobbles to ~/Library/Application Support/ScrobbleNow/
- Retries pending scrobbles every 5 minutes (up to 10 attempts per entry)
- Logs successful/failed scrobble history (last 1000 entries)
- Caps pending queue at 500 entries
- Stats: pendingCount, successCount, failedCount
- Clear methods for pending and history

Integration:
- Successful scrobbles logged to history
- Failed scrobbles queued for retry automatically
- Retry timer runs every 5 minutes in background
- Total scrobbled count updates after successful retries

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Add expanded Now Playing, smart filtering, settings overhaul, artwork fetching

Expanded Now Playing (iOS Music-style):
- Tap compact card to expand into full-screen artwork view
- 240px album art with shadow, large title/artist/album
- Full progress bar, source app badge, scrobble progress ring
- Action buttons: Love, Discover, Edit
- Multi-source list below when multiple apps playing
- Smooth spring animation expand/collapse

Smart Filtering:
- YouTube Music API check (musicVideoType) for music vs non-music
- YouTube page category fallback (regex "category":"Music")
- All MUSIC_VIDEO_ types now return artist/track (not just OMV)
- Non-music YouTube videos silently skipped

Artwork Fetching:
- YouTube thumbnails (img.youtube.com/vi/{id}/hqdefault.jpg)
- Last.fm album.getInfo artwork
- Last.fm track.getInfo fallback
- Last.fm artist.getInfo as final fallback

Settings Overhaul:
- Scrobble Behavior section: threshold slider, min duration, podcast filter,
  album guessing, notifications toggle, live scrobble stats
- Sources section: per-app connector toggles with app icons, auto-discovered

commit.message:
Normalize UI repository to staging/release deployment model

Rework the UI repository around a two-environment branch model where staging is the integration branch and release is the controlled production branch. Update the Cloudflare Pages deploy workflow to target only those branches, resolve branch-specific Pages projects and backend endpoints from GitHub variables, and fail fast when required environment configuration is missing.

Fix frontend runtime backend selection so injected environment config is honored instead of forcing production API traffic, and align the core dependency sync workflow with the actual deployment build so new core publishes can continue to update and redeploy staging automatically.

Document the full deployment model, required repository variables and secrets, Cloudflare Pages configuration, and manual cleanup steps. Remove legacy branch references from repository files, move the npm publish workflow to manual dispatch so it does not interfere with the new release branch behavior, rename the local production branch to release, and delete the local deprecated default branch after verifying it contained no unique commits.

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

Add native macOS scrobbling via MediaRemote + browser detection

System-wide Now Playing detection:
- MediaRemote framework bridge for Spotify, Apple Music, and native apps
- DistributedNotificationCenter for instant Spotify/Music track changes
- osascript subprocess for Chrome/Safari tab scanning (YouTube, SoundCloud,
  Bandcamp, Tidal, Deezer, Spotify Web)
- Multi-source support — shows ALL playing sources simultaneously
- 1s polling for MediaRemote, 3s for browsers with 2s timeout

Scrobble engine (SystemScrobbleService):
- Track change detection with elapsed time tracking
- Scrobble rules: >50% duration OR >4min (configurable)
- Per-app connector system with auto-discovery
- updateNowPlaying + scrobble to Last.fm

UI:
- System Now Playing card with source app icon, scrobble progress ring,
  playback progress bar, and green checkmark on scrobble
- Multiple source cards shown simultaneously
- NSAppleEventsUsageDescription for Chrome automation permission

TODO: Smart filtering (music vs non-music), scrobble cache, expanded view

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Stop retries for gone actors

Handle Fedify 2.1.0 unverified Delete activities whose signing key
fetch now returns 410 Gone by acknowledging the request with 202
Accepted. This prevents remote servers from repeatedly redelivering
deletes for actors that are already permanently gone.

Keep these unverified activities out of the normal BotKit event
handler flow, add regression coverage for the decision logic, and
document the behavior in the changelog and events guide.

https://github.com/fedify-dev/fedify/issues/472
https://fedify.dev/manual/inbox

Co-Authored-By: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix(browser): Chrome 146 CDP dialog handling + faster response polling (#86)

Chrome 146 shows an "Allow remote debugging?" dialog on each new
external CDP connection, breaking automated recipe flows. This patch:

- Bounds the auto-connect probe to 15s (was unbounded/hanging)
- Reuses healthy existing daemon connections (skips probe entirely)
- Detects "awaiting approval" state and tells user to click Allow
  instead of silently falling back to a separate browser without login
- Skips daemon close for live-attach in auth checks
- Force-kills only truly stale daemons (grace window for recent PIDs)

Also improves ChatGPT response polling:
- Poll interval: 15s → 5s (3x faster detection)
- Total deadline: new --timeout-ms with 3-min default
- Better error reporting (elapsed time, deadline vs attempt limit)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: expose raw unrolled proof access (#34)

## Summary
- re-export `UnrolledProgramProof` from `airbender-host::raw` as a raw
API without stability guarantees
- keep `RealProof::into_inner()` so callers can extract the wrapped
proof when they need direct access
- document that direct use of the raw proof is not recommended and is
intended only for rare integration cases

## Testing
- `cargo fmt --all`
- `cargo check -p airbender-host --no-default-features`
- `cargo nextest run -p airbender-host --no-default-features`

## Notes
- `cargo check -p airbender-host` and `cargo nextest run -p
airbender-host` require the CUDA toolkit in this environment because
`gpu-prover` is a default feature; they fail here before reaching crate
validation.

---------

build use `--locked`

that build the guest and run the host `--prove` flow through the
examples' dev prover path

crates/cargo-airbender --locked --force --debug`

--locked`

examples/cycle-markers/host/Cargo.toml --locked`

Google Merchant and OpenAI feed routes. Extract cached data fetching into
dedicated `feed-data.ts` modules using `cacheLife('products')` and

Summary:

- reorganize the content into Start Here, Concepts, Console, Developer, and Reference sections
- add richer visuals and MDX composition, plus update docs tooling and ignores for the generated source

Rationale:
- align the docs experience with the requested full-feature Fumadocs structure and component model
- remove the duplicate Vitepress source tree so the repository only carries one documentation system forward

Tests:

- corepack pnpm -C docs exec eslint .

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(docs): harden fumadocs runtime setup

Summary:
- add the missing Node types dependency and refresh the docs lockfile
- remove the stale ImageZoom stylesheet import that broke Next dev
- align the docs tsconfig and Next config with the current Next.js
  expectations for the Fumadocs app

Rationale:
- the migration already rendered cleanly under static checks, but the docs
  app still failed at runtime under next dev
- fixing the runtime setup closes the last defect in the Fumadocs
  migration and keeps the local docs workflow reproducible

Tests:

* fix(docs): align fumadocs home with origin design

Summary:
- replace the custom homepage redesign with a token-based Fumadocs landing
  that follows the origin/main narrative and section structure
- remove custom font loading and bespoke gradient/background styling from the
  docs app shell
- keep the shared MDX helper styles, but restyle them with standard Fumadocs
  theme tokens

Rationale:
- the pulled branch tip drifted from the ticket guardrails by introducing new
  typography and background treatment instead of preserving the existing docs
  design direction
- moving the landing back onto built-in theme tokens keeps the migration in
  bounds while retaining the richer Fumadocs docs structure

- remove the documentation hub page and promote top-level docs folders

- scope sidebar navigation to the active section and remove duplicate H1
  headings from doc bodies
- rename Start Here to Getting Started and rewrite Quickstart and

commit.message:
fix(feed): canonicalize cache tags + OpenAI manage_stock (#852)

* fix(feed): canonicalize cache tags to merchant UUID + OpenAI manage_stock

- Restructure both feed routes (Google Merchant + OpenAI) to resolve
  the merchant outside `unstable_cache` so cache tags always use
  canonical merchant.id (UUID), not slugs. This fixes a cache
  invalidation blind spot where slug-based and UUID-based entries
  could diverge.

- Add `manage_stock` to OpenAI feed route's product select and type.
  When `manage_stock === false`, simple products now correctly emit
  `in_stock` with quantity 9999 (unlimited stock).

- Create shared `feed-identifier.ts` with `resolveFeedMerchant()` and
  `isUuid()` for reuse across feed routes and revalidation.

- Update revalidation endpoint to accept `{ merchant_id }` (preferred)
  alongside legacy `{ identifier }`. Response shape changes from
  `{ revalidated, identifier }` to `{ revalidated, merchant_id }`.

- Update backfill script to send `{ merchant_id }` instead of slug.

- Standardize both feed routes to use `createAnonClient()` singleton.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(feed): address CodeRabbit review findings

- Mock @/lib/cache-headers in feed route tests (worktree resolution)
- Add error body assertion to revalidation 500 test
- Add response.status assertion to OpenAI cache tag test
- Use path alias @/lib/feed-identifier in test import
- Preserve Supabase error cause in feed-identifier throw
- Add merchant_id positive-path test for OpenAI route

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(feed): add MerchantNotFoundError, Zod validation, resolveFeedMerchant tests

- Add MerchantNotFoundError class to feed-identifier.ts; separate DB
  errors (throw generic Error) from "not found" (throw typed error)
- Replace fragile string matching on error.message with instanceof
  checks in all three route handlers (GMC, OpenAI, revalidate)
- Add Zod schema to OpenAI route for query param validation (aligns
  with existing GMC pattern); validates merchant_id as UUID, format
  as enum
- Add resolveFeedMerchant tests: slug resolution, UUID resolution,
  MerchantNotFoundError on missing merchant, generic Error on DB failure

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
ci(tests): give backend suite more headroom

Summary:
- raise the backend test step timeout from 10 to 20 minutes on GitHub Actions
- switch the backend pytest invocation from -v to -q to reduce runner log overhead while keeping failure summaries

Rationale:
- the Wave 3 backend suite is passing locally but is too close to the old 10 minute budget on GitHub runners
- quieter output plus more timeout headroom prevents false CI failures from runner slowness rather than test regressions

Tests:
- git diff --check
- Hooke review: no findings

commit.message:
fix: restore corollary 1.3 to the Euler-form layer

Undo the over-generalization from the previous proposition-object rename.
Corollary 1.3 is a statement about the Euler form, not an arbitrary bilinear
form `χ`, so the proposition-object should live in the Euler layer and carry
that specialization explicitly.

- move `NumericalStabilityCondition.CentralChargeIsLocalHomeomorphOnConnectedComponents`
  back to `EulerForm.lean`
- delete the incorrect generic `χ`-parametrized proposition-object from
  `NumericalStability.lean`
- keep the manifold packaging aligned with the Euler-specialized statement
- sync the README and statement audit to the restored abstraction boundary

Build and lint passed:
- lake exe cache get
- lake build BridgelandStability
- lake exe runLinter

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Summary:
- require lifecycle approval whenever a password-backed config field is explicitly supplied with a new value, including null and other non-string payloads
- keep redacted/no-op reconfigure requests approval-free
- add a regression covering the null-secret approval path and unchanged redacted reconfigure

commit.message:
refactor: rename the theorem 1.2 and 1.3 proposition objects

Rename the proposition objects for Bridgeland's Theorem 1.2 and Corollary 1.3
so they follow the intended `UpperCamelCase` convention for named `Prop`
objects, and move the numerical local-homeomorphism proposition into the
`NumericalStabilityCondition` namespace.

- rename Theorem 1.2 to
  `StabilityCondition.CentralChargeIsLocalHomeomorphOnConnectedComponents`
- rename the assembled proof theorem to
  `StabilityCondition.centralChargeIsLocalHomeomorphOnConnectedComponents`
- replace `bridgelandCorollary_1_3` with
  `NumericalStabilityCondition.CentralChargeIsLocalHomeomorphOnConnectedComponents`
- rename the manifold theorem to
  `NumericalStabilityCondition.existsComplexManifoldOnConnectedComponent`
- sync the README and audit documents to the new proposition-object API

Build and lint passed:
- lake exe cache get
- lake build BridgelandStability
- lake exe runLinter

refactor: rename public proof-scaffolding declarations

Continue the naming cleanup by replacing several public declarations whose
names still exposed proof scaffolding rather than mathematical content.
Also sync the README and naming audit to reflect the current theorem names
and the remaining naming debt.

- rename `StabilityCondition.False_of_HN_phases_le_with_lt` to
  `StabilityCondition.false_of_hn_phases_le_with_lt`
- rename `Slicing.tStructureAux` and `Slicing.tStructureAuxGE` to
  `Slicing.exists_triangle_gtProp_leProp` and
  `Slicing.exists_triangle_geProp_ltProp`
- rename `gtProp_of_geProp_of_lt` to `Slicing.gtProp_of_geProp`
- update downstream references in the deformation and topology modules
- sync `README.md` and `artifacts/mathlib-naming-audit.md`

commit.message:
Work (#5)

* Revamp dashboard, clients and appointments UI; add Conversations & Automations pages; reorganize settings/system/sidebar

### Motivation
- Simplify and refocus several admin screens to surface operational actions and reduce clutter from deep analytics and rarely-used filters.
- Provide compact, action-oriented summaries and visualizations so teams can quickly spot issues (AI handoffs, cancellations, errors) and act.
- Introduce two new working screens for monitoring dialog flow (`conversations`) and automation scenarios (`automations`).
- Reorganize navigation and settings to separate core MVP actions from legacy/secondary functionality.

### Description
- Reworked the dashboard (`app/page.tsx`) to show AI-centric KPIs and small charts rendered with `recharts`, added automation summary cards, and simplified recent-activity rendering; replaced several legacy charts/components with compact area/bar charts and attention cards.
- Rebuilt the appointments view (`app/appointments/page.tsx`) to surface operational status cards, a status bar chart, and an inline simplified table instead of the previous full-featured table and funnel/heatmap visualization.
- Simplified the clients screen (`app/clients/page.tsx`) by removing a large set of advanced filters and campaign UI, replacing them with lightweight actionable filters, summary metric cards, and a concise client table with scenario tags and next-appointment info.
- Added `app/conversations/page.tsx` to monitor inbound dialog flow, channel distribution and AI vs human handling with chart and list views.
- Added `app/automations/page.tsx` to present scenario metrics, health indicators and simple controls for automation scenarios.
- Updated system and settings pages (`app/system/page.tsx`, `app/settings/page.tsx`) to reorganize sections, add anchors/IDs for intra-page navigation, and surface core controls (agent toggle, knowledge uploads, roles, notifications) with clearer labels.
- Updated the sidebar (`components/layout/Sidebar.tsx`) to reflect new navigation structure, introduce a small "Future / Legacy" section for less-used links, and tune item rendering behavior for anchor/hash links.
- Misc: removed several complex helper utilities and UI modules from these pages, introduced `formatDate`/`formatCurrency` usages, adjusted icons and color palettes, and added small UX text/content changes across pages.

### Testing
- Performed a local type-check with `tsc --noEmit`, which completed successfully.
- Built the application with `yarn build` and verified the production build step completed without errors.
- Ran linting (`yarn lint`) and unit test command (`yarn test`) where available; these commands completed without reported failures.

* Restore data-driven admin screens and navigation

* Adjust settings page layout

* Move auto-systems section to settings

---------

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix: use 127.0.0.1 instead of localhost for redirect URI

Spotify now rejects localhost — requires explicit loopback IP.

Co-authored-by: codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Fix party expense log pagination and loading loops (#133)

* fix(pwa): stabilize party expense log pagination

Summary:
- extract party expense pagination into a dedicated library with
  imperative loaders, stable snapshots, and newest-first expense
  ordering
- replace the buggy hook implementation with a Suspense-friendly
  wrapper and preload the visible expense chunks from the party route
- add unit and Playwright coverage for chunk pagination, live updates,
  and newly created expenses returning to the top of the log

Rationale:
- the previous hook could trigger infinite update loops and was hard to
  reuse safely for preloading and Suspense integration
- moving the pagination state outside the hook makes the loading model
  predictable and gives us focused tests around chunk behavior

Tests:
- pnpm lingui:extract
- pnpm lint
- pnpm typecheck
- pnpm test
- pnpm --filter @trizum/pwa exec playwright test e2e/expense-log.spec.ts
  --project=chromium --workers=1 --reporter=line
- PLAYWRIGHT_BASE_URL=http://localhost:5173 pnpm --filter @trizum/pwa
  exec playwright test e2e/expense-log.spec.ts --project=chromium
  --workers=1 --reporter=line

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(pwa): stop resuspending the expense log after mount

Summary:
- remove the local ExpenseLog Suspense boundary from the party route
- keep already loaded expenses visible while new leading chunks load in
  the background
- make the expense pagination hook non-suspending and extend the unit
  coverage for leading-chunk updates

Rationale:
- once the party route has mounted we do not want new synced chunks to
  bounce the whole screen back into a pending state
- the route loader already preloads the visible expense page, so the
  hook can safely switch to background loading for follow-up updates

Tests:
- pnpm --filter @trizum/pwa exec vitest run src/lib/partyPaginatedExpenses.test.ts
- pnpm --filter @trizum/pwa typecheck
- pnpm --filter @trizum/pwa exec eslint src/hooks/usePartyPaginatedExpenses.ts src/lib/partyPaginatedExpenses.ts src/lib/partyPaginatedExpenses.test.ts 'src/routes/party.$partyId.tsx'
- PLAYWRIGHT_BASE_URL=http://localhost:5173 pnpm --filter @trizum/pwa exec playwright test e2e/expense-log.spec.ts --project=chromium --workers=1 --reporter=line

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* style: apply automatic fixes

Signed-off-by: GitHub Actions <actions@github.com>

* test(pwa): extract document cache test helper

Summary:
- add a reusable mock document cache collection for PWA unit tests
- move party expense pagination tests onto the shared cache helper
- preserve the existing expense-specific chunk builders in the test file

Rationale:
- reuse the cache double in other tests without copying subscription and
  status logic
- keep pagination tests focused on pagination behavior instead of cache
  plumbing

Tests:
- pnpm --filter @trizum/pwa exec vitest run src/lib/partyPaginatedExpenses.test.ts

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

---------

Signed-off-by: GitHub Actions <actions@github.com>
Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
Co-authored-by: HorusGoul <HorusGoul@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(feed): add missing stock_quantity and manage_stock to GMC feed query (#851)

The Google Merchant Center feed route was selecting only the legacy
`stock` column, ignoring `stock_quantity` and `manage_stock`. This
caused products with manage_stock=false to appear out-of-stock (instead
of unlimited/9999) and products using the modern stock_quantity field to
fall back to the legacy stock value (often 0).

Extracts the select projection into a testable constant in feed-query.ts
and adds route-level regression tests that capture the actual select
argument and assert both columns are present.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
